Terrace Archive

A lifelong football collection, catalogued and priced for a one-off sale - 1,100+ programmes, tickets and memorabilia spanning five decades of Scottish and English football.

Home By Team Memorabilia By Year By Competition Signed Items The McCathie Story Bundles Job Lots How to Buy

FP-0109

Carlisle United vs Liverpool

Programme · 1974-75

Football League Division One

Available

Carlisle United's home programme for the visit of Liverpool to Brunton Park, 5 October 1974, from the Cumbrians' only season in the top flight. Carlisle famously began that campaign top of Division One, and programmes from their solitary year among the elite are steadily collected by Carlisle supporters and completists alike. Priced 10p, with a diving-goalkeeper action photo on the cover.

Buy now - £2.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0109 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0211

Middlesbrough vs Sheffield United

Programme · 1970-71

Football League Division Two

Signed Available

Middlesbrough's official programme for the Division Two match against Sheffield United at Ayresome Park, 24 April 1971, with the ball graphic on the front cover filled with handwritten signatures. They appear to be autographs of the 1970-71 Boro squad - names consistent with Jim Platt, Willie Maddren, Stuart Boam and John Hickton can be made out - but the set is unauthenticated, so judge the close-up photographs for yourself.

Buy now - £4

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0211 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0318

Newcastle United vs Liverpool

Programme · 1995-96

FA Carling Premiership

Signed Available

Newcastle United v Liverpool official programme from 4 November 1995, deep in the Keegan title-challenge season that Tyneside still talks about. Inside is a Magpie Collection centrefold poster of Peter Beardsley carrying what appears to be his signature in blue ink - unauthenticated, so judge the close-up photographs for yourself. A strong package for collectors of the 1995-96 Entertainers side.

Buy now - £3.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0318 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0322

Newcastle United vs Queens Park Rangers

Programme · 1982-83

Football League Division Two

Available

The famous 'We Have Got Kevin!' issue - Newcastle United's official match magazine for the visit of Queens Park Rangers in August 1982, celebrating Kevin Keegan's astonishing signing from Southampton with the England captain and the club chairman on the cover. Keegan scored on his debut in this match, and the programme has become the definitive keepsake of the moment that transformed the club. Vol 6 No 1, priced 35p.

Buy now - £17

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0322 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0368

Newcastle United vs Grimsby Town

Programme · 1982-83

Football League Division Two

Signed Available

Home matchday programme for Newcastle United v Grimsby Town in Division Two, played at St James' Park on 2 April 1983 - Newcastle won 4-0, with goals from Kevin Keegan, Imre Varadi (two) and Neil McDonald. The 'Home and Away' back page carries Newcastle Brown Ale's standard advert; the '1927-1977' on it refers to the ale's own 50th anniversary, not the match. A clear autograph in blue ink runs across the top of the cover; the signer is unidentified and unauthenticated, so it's offered as the programme with the autograph as a talking point.

Buy now - £3.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0368 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0400

Newcastle United vs Crystal Palace

Programme · 1981-82

Football League Division Two

Possibly signed Available

Home matchday programme for Newcastle United v Crystal Palace in Division Two, played at St James' Park on 31 March 1982 (a 0-0 draw). The 'Home and Away' back page carries Newcastle Brown Ale's standard advert; the '1927-1977' on it is the ale's own 50th anniversary, not the match date. A faint pencil/pen mark crosses the top of the cover; it may be an autograph but is too indistinct to attribute, so it's offered simply as the programme.

Buy now - £3.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0400 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0403

Newcastle United vs Leeds United

Programme · 1975-76

Football League Division One

Signed Available

Team Check page from a Newcastle United v Leeds United Division One programme, 1975-76, listing two heavyweight line-ups of the era - Macdonald, Gowling and Kennedy for Newcastle; Bremner, Clarke and Jordan for Leeds. A clear ink autograph runs across the Newcastle team box; it may be Malcolm Macdonald, but it is unconfirmed and unauthenticated, so judge the close-up photographs for yourself.

Buy now - £3.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0403 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0409

Newcastle United vs Oldham Athletic

Programme · 1970s

Football League

Signed Available

Newcastle United official matchday magazine v Oldham Athletic (Vol 3 No 2), with a full first-team squad photograph on the cover. Look closely at the sky above the players' heads and you'll find four or five handwritten autographs in blue ink - plausibly a matchday signing session, with names suggesting Kelly, Davies and Cartwright among the possibilities. None are conclusively identified and the set is unauthenticated, so the close-up photographs are there for you to make your own judgement.

Buy now - £3.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0409 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0411

Newcastle United vs West Ham United

Programme · 1977-78

Football League Division One

Signed Available

Back-page 'Home and Away' team-sheet from a Newcastle United v West Ham United Division One programme, complete with both line-ups and a Newcastle Brown Ale advert marking the brew's 1927-1977 golden jubilee. Two handwritten autographs sit in the sky above the bottle, one partially legible and possibly ending in 'Hewitt' - neither is identified or authenticated, so please treat them as an intriguing extra rather than the reason to buy.

Buy now - £3.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0411 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

2 photos
FP-0491

Manchester United vs Arsenal

Programme · 1971-72

Football League Division One

Available

A genuine oddity: the Anfield Review Special Issue covering Manchester United v Arsenal, Division One, 20 August 1971. United had been ordered to play their first two home games of the season away from Old Trafford following crowd trouble, and staged this fixture at Anfield - so Liverpool's own programme covered a United home win over Arsenal. Photographed with the team-sheet page and an original green match ticket for the same fixture. A curious footnote of football history with appeal to followers of all three clubs.

Buy now - £3.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0491 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0520

Aberdeen vs Dunfermline

Programme · 1996-97

Bells League Championship Premier Division

Signed Available

The Dons matchday programme for Aberdeen v Dunfermline Athletic, Premier Division, 4 January 1997. The cover carries at least three autographs in blue ink across the player photo and masthead - believed to be members of the 1996-97 Aberdeen squad, though the signers are unidentified and the signatures are unauthenticated. Sold exactly as seen.

Buy now - £3.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0520 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0527

Airdrieonians vs Dunfermline Athletic

Programme · 1993-94

Scottish League

Available

The Broomfield Bulletin, issue 30 of season 1993-94, titled 'The End of an Era': Airdrieonians v Dunfermline Athletic, 7 May 1994 - the final match ever played at Broomfield Park after 102 years. Last-game-at-the-old-ground programmes are a collecting niche of their own, and this is the farewell issue for one of Scottish football's most distinctive old grounds.

Buy now - £2

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0527 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

Signed Available

Sons View programme for Dumbarton v Dunfermline Athletic, Scottish League First Division, Boghead Park, 6 November 1993. The illustrated cover carries two handwritten autographs in blue ink, one with a 'Best wishes' dedication; the signers have not been identified and the signatures are unauthenticated. Offered exactly as found.

Buy now - £2

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0618 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

Dumbarton vs Ayr United

Programme · 1992-93

Scottish League Division One

Signed Available

Sons View programme for Dumbarton v Ayr United, Scottish League Division One, Boghead Park, 19 September 1992. The cover bears a handwritten dedication in green ink - 'Best Wishes' followed by a signature that appears to read MacPherson - though the signer has not been identified and the signature is unauthenticated.

Programme · 1989-90

B&Q League Premier League

Possibly signed Available

Heart of Midlothian programme (Vol.4 No.18) for the Premier League fixture against Dunfermline Athletic at Tynecastle, 10 February 1990. There is a handwritten annotation in the top right corner of the cover, possibly a name and a scrawled mark below it - unidentified and unauthenticated. A solid Tynecastle issue from the Pars' top-flight days.

FP-0770

St Mirren vs Dunfermline Athletic

Programme · 1995-96

Bells First Division

Available

St Mirren official matchday magazine for the Bell's First Division fixture against Dunfermline Athletic at Love Street, 6 January 1996. Two days after this match, Dunfermline's long-serving captain Norrie McCathie died, and the original owner has written on the cover in blue ink: 'Norrie McCathie's last ever game for the Pars before his tragic death.' The annotation marks the cover, but it also records exactly why this ordinary away programme became something more. A quiet, poignant piece of Dunfermline history.

Buy now - £2.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0770 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0795

Dunfermline Athletic vs Aberdeen

Programme · 1994-95

Scottish Premier Division Play-Off

Includes matching ticketPart of a bundle → Available

The Pars Programme (No. 23, season 1994/95) for the Premier Division play-off against Aberdeen at East End Park, Thursday 25 May 1995 - the second leg of the first promotion/relegation play-off in Scottish top-flight history, played before a 16,000 crowd. Aberdeen survived, avoiding relegation in what remains a unique episode for both clubs. A notable one-off occasion that rarely appears for sale.

Buy now - £4

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0795 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

FP-0804

Dunfermline Athletic vs Queen of the South

Programme · 1984-85

Scottish League Second Division

Signed Available

Dunfermline Athletic match magazine for the Second Division fixture against Queen of the South at East End Park, 29 September 1984, bearing multiple handwritten autographs across the front cover. The signers have not been identified and the signatures are unauthenticated. Photographed together with an original Dunfermline Athletic team-sheet for the home match against Celtic on 23 January 1988, listing both starting line-ups.

Dunfermline Athletic v Falkirk, Irn-Bru First Division, East End Park, 7 May 2011 - the Champions Souvenir Issue celebrating the Pars' First Division title win of 2010-11. Bells First Division

£1.50 sold

Sold

Dunfermline Athletic v Clydebank, Bell's First Division, East End Park, Saturday 13 January 1996 - the tribute issue published days after the death of club captain Norrie McCathie, with the cover dedicated to him: 23 March 1961 - 8 January 1996, 'Mr Dunfermline'. For Pars supporters, this is among the most meaningful programmes the club ever produced.

2 photos
FP-0917

Dunfermline Athletic vs Clyde

Programme · 1993-94

Scottish League First Division

Signed Part of a bundle → Available

Official Dunfermline Athletic programme (No. 25) for the final home game of the 1993-94 season, against Clyde at East End Park on 14 May 1994. This copy was signed by several players after the match - a clear autograph across the cover photo and further signatures on the inside team page, around four or five in total. The signers have not been identified and the signatures are unauthenticated; sold exactly as photographed.

Buy now - £2.50

Tap to email me and reserve FP-0917 - I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details (a simple bank transfer). UK postage is included in the price.

Interested in an item or bundle?
Each one has a Buy Now button that opens an email with the item ID filled in - send it and I'll confirm it's still available and reply with payment details. You can also email terracearchive@icloud.com directly with the item ID (e.g. FP-0042 or MEM-0011) if you have a question first, or to buy several items in one go. Also open to job-lot offers - the whole collection, all programmes, or any other grouping you'd like - get in touch to discuss.
Terrace Archive · private catalogue link, not indexed for search engines.
